classObjective: Identify a single transformation that is equivalent to a composition of two reflections.
workedExamples: 13-4, Example 3 — Identify a Single Transformation Equivalent to Two Reflections in Parallel Lines (Translation)
13-4, Example 4 — Identify a Single Transformation Equivalent to Two Reflections in Intersecting Lines (Rotation)
13-4, Example 5 — Determine Congruence and Find a Composition of Transformations
warmUp: Two parallel lines are 4 units apart. What single transformation equals reflecting across both?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: two parallel lines → translation (2d distance); two intersecting lines → rotation (2θ); congruence from compositions
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice identifying single transformations equivalent to reflection compositions
independentPractice: Practice problems determining what single transformation equals given reflection compositions
assessment: Exit ticket: what single transformation is equivalent to reflection across two parallel lines 3 units apart?
capReflection: How does knowing that two reflections compose to a translation or rotation help you understand the nature of rigid motions?
